tools.name: decompile-from-path
tools.description: Decompiles a Java .class file from a given file path

classFilePath.description: The absolute path to the .class file

tools.name: decompile-from-package
tools.description: Decompiles a Java class from a package name

packageName.description: Fully qualified Java package and class name

classpath.description: Array of classpath directories to search

tools.name: decompile-from-jar
tools.description:
Decompiles a Java class from a JAR file

# Using mcp_javadc with Maven Repository

When you need to decompile Java classes from dependencies in the M2 repository, follow these steps:

## Step 1: Find the JAR file location

First, search for the dependency JAR in the local Maven repository:

```bash
find ~/.m2 -name "*dependency-name*jar" | grep -v source | grep -v javadoc
```

Notes:
- Replace dependency-name with the artifact name
- Filter out source and javadoc JARs using grep
- Look for the correct version based on the project's POM file

## Step 2: Use the correct mcp_javadc function

Once you have the JAR path, use this function:

For specific class decompilation:
- jarFilePath: The absolute path to the JAR (from Step 1)
- className: Fully qualified class name to decompile

For contextual exploration:
If needed, first try to find all available classes in the JAR:
`jar tf /path/to/the.jar | grep .class | sort`

Example workflow:
1. Read the POM file to identify dependency version
2. Search the M2 repository for the JAR
3. Use mcp_javadc to decompile relevant classes
4. If multiple versions exist, select the one matching the project's version requirement

jarFilePath.description: The absolute path to the JAR file

className.description: Fully qualified class name to decompile from the JAR (e.g., "com.example.MyClass")

findings.title: Excessive description length in 'decompile-from-jar'
findings.description: Description is 1175 chars (limit 1000). May contain hidden content.
findings.remediation: Keep descriptions concise; review for hidden instructions.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'decompile-from-path.classFilePath'
findings.description: OS error response to traversal payload — the path was passed to the filesystem (field: path). File content not leaked, but path traversal is confirmed.
findings.evidence: Error: Failed to decompile class file: ENOENT: no such file or directory, access 'C:\Proyectos\CobaltoSec\etc\shadow'
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'decompile-from-package.packageName'
findings.description: Payload was reflected verbatim in the response without sanitization (field classification: generic_string).
findings.evidence: Error: Failed to decompile package: Could not find class file for package: <script>alert(1)</script>
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: 'decompile-from-path' accepts missing required fields
findings.description: Tool succeeded with empty arguments despite required: ['classFilePath']
findings.remediation: Validate that all required parameters are present before processing.

findings.title: 'decompile-from-path.classFilePath' silently accepts wrong type
findings.description: Expected type 'string', sent NoneType value 'None' — no error returned.
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'decompile-from-path' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: 'decompile-from-package' accepts missing required fields
findings.description: Tool succeeded with empty arguments despite required: ['packageName']
findings.remediation: Validate that all required parameters are present before processing.

findings.title: 'decompile-from-package.packageName' silently accepts wrong type
findings.description: Expected type 'string', sent NoneType value 'None' — no error returned.
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'decompile-from-package' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: 'decompile-from-jar' accepts missing required fields

findings.remediation: Validate that all required parameters are present before processing.

findings.title: 'decompile-from-jar.jarFilePath' silently accepts wrong type
findings.description: Expected type 'string', sent NoneType value 'None' — no error returned.
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'decompile-from-jar' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.
